message:
fix(db): preserve phage catalog order by sorting on id instead of name

Changes the ORDER BY clause from `name ASC` to `id ASC` in both the Bun
SQLite repository and the sql.js web repository to maintain the original
phage catalog insertion order.

Motivation:
- The PHAGE_CATALOG in data-pipeline defines phages in a pedagogically
  meaningful order, with Lambda (the classic temperate phage) first
- Sorting alphabetically by name disrupted this ordering, placing less
  familiar phages before foundational examples
- Lambda is the canonical starting point for phage biology education and
  should appear first in the list

Technical Details:
- packages/db-runtime/src/bun-repository.ts: Update listPhages() Drizzle
  query to use `orderBy(asc(phages.id))`
- packages/web/src/db/SqlJsRepository.ts: Update prepared statement SQL
  from `ORDER BY name ASC` to `ORDER BY id ASC`

This ensures users see phages in the curated order: Lambda, T4, T7,
PhiX174, MS2, M13, P22, Phi29, Mu, Phi6, SPbeta, T5 - matching the
order they appear in documentation and the build pipeline.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@ export class SqlJsRepository implements PhageRepository {
         SELECT id, slug, name, accession, family, host, genome_length as genomeLength,
                gc_content as gcContent, morphology, lifecycle
         FROM phages
-        ORDER BY name ASC
+        ORDER BY id ASC
       `),
 
       getPhageById: this.db.prepare(`
